key_points: Electric current flows only when a circuit forms a complete closed path
Cells provide the electrical energy required for the flow of current
Circuit components include cells, wires, switches and electrical devices such as bulbs
Current flows from the positive terminal of the cell to the negative terminal through the circuit
Materials are classified as conductors or insulators depending on their ability to allow current flow
examples: Closed circuit: Cell → wire → bulb → switch ON → bulb glows
Open circuit: Switch OFF → current cannot flow
Series connection: Two bulbs connected one after another in a single path
Testing materials: Using a circuit to check whether a material conducts electricity
formulas: Electric current → Flow of electric charges through a conductor
Conductor → Material that allows electric current to pass through easily
Insulator → Material that prevents the flow of electric current
exam_tips: Understand how current flows in a complete circuit
Learn the function of each component in a circuit
Identify conductors and insulators in everyday objects
